agent.name: James Okonkwo
agent.notable_actions: Restored 42-day streak by manually marking the class complete and added device to engineering ticket as a repeat-issue case.

resolution.summary: Streak restored and crash logged, but underlying Samsung TV app instability persists until the mid-March update.

key_moments.trigger: Concrete fix in-call rather than promise of future fix

key_moments.description: Customer learns fix is still weeks away
key_moments.trigger: Mid-March update timeline given for a recurring crash

pain_points.description: Samsung TV app crashes mid-class on 2021 model (fourth time in one month)
pain_points.verbatim_quote: the whole app just froze and then kicked me out.

pain_points.description: Crashes cause loss of workout streak; customer must call support to restore
pain_points.verbatim_quote: Forty-two days in a row and it broke because your app crashed.

pain_points.description: Customer cannot keep calling for each crash recovery

motivations.description: Daily class consistency is core to engagement

feature_requests.feature: Automatic streak protection when the app crashes mid-class
feature_requests.existing_workaround: Call support each time to manually restore

intent.primary: Restore lost workout streak after app crash
intent.secondary: Pressure for a faster fix on Samsung TV crashes

factors: Repeat crashes (4 in one month) on a paid feature
Mid-March fix timeline puts further crashes in the customer's path
Customer already planning a workaround (cast from phone)

action_items.action: Add device to Samsung TV repeat-issue ticket
Ship Samsung TV app fix (mid-March target)

review_reasons: Customer name, tenure, and plan not captured
Class title and exact crash count partially inaudible
